zh.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Charles Payne於1395年出生在英國諾福爾可時，他的父親Esquire William Robet De Payne48歲，母親Lady Joan De Tyas47歲。他於1420年與Agnes Kate Middleton在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1439年11月24日在英國肯特福利頓登享年44歲。

zh-hans.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Charles Payne于1395年出生在英国諾福爾可时，他的父亲Esquire William Robet De Payne48岁，母亲Lady Joan De Tyas47岁。他于1420年与Agnes Kate Middleton在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1439年11月24日在英国肯特福利頓登享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When John Charles Payne was born in 1395, in Norfolk, England, his father, Esquire William Robet De Payne, was 48 and his mother, Lady Joan De Tyas, was 47. He married Agnes Kate Middleton in 1420, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 24 November 1439, in Frittenden, Kent, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Charles Payne est né en 1395, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Esquire William Robet De Payne, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan De Tyas, avait 47 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Kate Middleton en 1420, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1439, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Charles Payne 1395 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Esquire William Robet De Payne 48 und seine Mutter Lady Joan De Tyas 47. Er hat 1420 in England, United Kingdom Agnes Kate Middleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 24. November 1439 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Frittenden, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Charles Payne è nato il nel 1395 nel Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Esquire William Robet De Payne, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Lady Joan De Tyas, aveva 47 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Kate Middleton nel 1420, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 24 novembre 1439, nel Frittenden, Kent, England, all'età di 44 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Charles Payne(이)가 1395년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Esquire William Robet De Payne(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joan De Tyas(은)는 47세였다. 그는 1420년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Agnes Kate Middleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1439년 11월 24일 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Charles Payne nasceu em 1395, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Esquire William Robet De Payne, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joan De Tyas, tinha 47 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Kate Middleton em 1420, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1439, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Charles Payne родился в 1395 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Esquire William Robet De Payne было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan De Tyas, 47. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Kate Middleton в 1420 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 24 ноября 1439 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Charles Payne nació en 1395, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Esquire William Robet De Payne, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Lady Joan De Tyas, tenía 47 años. Se casó con Agnes Kate Middleton en 1420, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1439, en Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
